title: Data Types Overview
description: Understand int, double, char, string, and bool.

slides.title: C++ Data Types 🧩
slides.content:
A variable in C++ must be a specified data type.

Here are the most basic ones:
* `int`: integer (whole numbers without decimals)
* `double`: floating point numbers (with decimals)
* `char`: single characters
* `string`: sequences of characters (text)
* `bool`: boolean values (true or false)

slides.title: Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
int myNum = 5;               // Integer
double myFloatNum = 5.99;    // Floating point number
char myLetter = 'D';         // Character
string myText = "Hello";     // String
bool myBoolean = true;       // Boolean

quizData.question: Which data type would you use to store the price of an item, like 19.99?

slides.title: Characters (char) 🅰️
slides.content:
The `char` data type is used to store a single character.

The character must be surrounded by **single quotes**, like `'A'` or `'c'`.

quizData.question: Which of these correctly creates a char variable?
quizData.explanation: chars must be surrounded by single quotes.

slides.title: Booleans (bool) ⚖️
slides.content:
A boolean data type is declared with the `bool` keyword and can only take the values `true` or `false`.

When the value is returned, `true` evaluates to `1` and `false` evaluates to `0`.

quizData.question: A boolean variable can only evaluate to true or false.
quizData.explanation: Booleans represent truth values, strictly true (1) or false (0).
